home.name: Tiancheng LIU
home.bio: Ph.D. Candidate at HKUST(GZ), Visiting Researcher at University of Amsterdam. I explore computational aesthetics, human-AI co-creativity, and culturally-grounded generative systems.

research.title: Research
research.subtitle: My work sits at the intersection of multimodal computing, embodied interaction, and generative AI for creative applications.

aesthetics.title: Computational Calligraphy Aesthetics
aesthetics.description: How can machines quantify and interpret the aesthetic qualities of Chinese calligraphy? From stroke-level features and personality correlations to full-composition layout analysis and seal carving evaluation.

embodied.title: Embodied Calligraphy & Reflective Practice
embodied.description: Exploring the embodied knowledge in calligraphy writing — micro-actions, body movements, emotional expression — and how technology can make these implicit processes perceivable for reflection and co-creation.

performance.title: Human-AI Co-Creative Performance
performance.description: Designing performance systems where humans and AI collaborate through everyday objects, embodied gestures, and generative techniques in music and art.

artwork.title: Artwork
artwork.subtitle: Exhibition works at the intersection of calligraphy, embodied interaction, and human-AI co-creation.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Thoughts on research, life, and everything in between.

about.title: About
about.story1: I’m Tiancheng LIU, a Ph.D. candidate in Computational Media and Arts at HKUST (Guangzhou), and currently a visiting researcher at the University of Amsterdam’s MultiX Lab.
about.story2: My research journey started from a rather different place — I studied Network Engineering and Finance at South China University of Technology, then spent two years at Waseda University in Tokyo working on blockchain and cloud computing. After joining China Telecom Research Institution as an engineer, I realized my true passion lay at the intersection of technology and culture.
about.story3: Now I work on computational interpretation of Chinese calligraphy, human-AI co-creative systems, and culturally-grounded generative AI. I’m fascinated by how machines can understand and participate in artistic creation while respecting cultural heritage.
about.beyond: I founded a 300+ member campus fitness community at HKUST(GZ). I play the bamboo flute, practice calligraphy, and enjoy trekking, triathlon, and urban exploration. I speak Mandarin, English, Cantonese, and Japanese.
about.connect: I’m always open to interesting conversations and collaboration opportunities.

cv.title: Curriculum Vitae
cv.description: Download my full CV for a detailed overview of my academic and professional background.
